semiology_text: SE, 22 months, followed by EPC, focal epilepsy with secondary generalization
mri_report_text: Initial MRI: Normal, repeat MRI: bilateral T2 high signal intensities in thalamic regions
eeg_report_text: Diffusely slow EEG with a very active left frontal spike and wave pattern

semiology_text: Severe neurologic disorder with hypotonia, abnormal movements, inability to swallow, intractable, probably myoclonic seizures, seizures manifest as Ohtahara syndrome, West syndrome, early myoclonic encephalopathy, or unspecified intractable seizures
mri_report_text: Severe hypoplasia of medulla, pons, and cerebellum, progressive cortical atrophy, simplified gyri, hypomyelination, pontocerebellar hypoplasia
eeg_report_text: Severely abnormal with multifocal abnormalities and slow discontinued and flat episodes

semiology_text: Clusters of short seizures with eye deviation, eyelid flickering, tonic arm extension, and back arching at 3 months; at 3 years 2 months, 10-20 epileptic seizures per day with varied semiology (episodic behavioural arrest, focal motor events, myoclonic jerks, brief tonic seizures with lower-limb extension); frequent paroxysms of laughter of undetermined origin, not captured on EEG
eeg_report_text: Ictal EEG showed generalized fast activity, more prominent over frontal regions; interictal EEG showed high-amplitude delta/theta multifocal (predominantly right anterior) discharges

semiology_text: Complex partial seizures (CPS) with frequency of 5/month, beginning with motionless staring and behavioral arrest, followed by oral alimentary or manual automatisms. Total CPS duration: 1-5 minutes. 6 patients reported auras: 2 strange but indescribable feeling, 1 smelled blood, 3 nervous feeling in heart.
mri_report_text: 3.0 T MRI confirmed enlarged amygdala (AE) on the right side. Enlarged amygdala iso- to slightly hyper-intense on 3.0 T FLAIR images, not enhanced by gadolinium. No hippocampal sclerosis (HS) suspected.
eeg_report_text: 24-hour EEG recordings with sphenoidal electrodes. Interictal spikes localized primarily to sphenoidal electrodes or anterior temporal-inferior frontal region (F7/F8). Unilateral IEDs ipsilateral to enlarged amygdala. Ictal EEG: seizure onset ipsilateral to AE, predominantly on anterior temporal electrodes and sphenoidal electrodes.

eeg_images: [{"path": "/mnt/anonymized/epilepsy_data_master/subfigures/mri/cropped_subfigures/bhu26202_subfig_00.png", "modality": "EEG_INTRACRANIAL", "caption": "(A) The results of language mapping with conventional 50 Hz cortical stimulation of vATL sites in Patients 1\u20135. Small black dots represent subdural electrodes investigated with cortical stimulation. Electrodes showing any language impairment are illustrated with large circles with 5 segments (one for each task). Filled segments denote impairment of the corresponding task by stimulation. A black fill denotes arrest by stimulation and gray fill denotes slowing or errors. The gray ovals mark the core language electrodes used as the site for the targeted stimulation Investigations 3 and 4 in these 3 patients. In this target area, high-frequency stimulation generated impairments across all the semantically related tasks (both receptive and expressive), but spared kana word reading (which requires visual processing and speech production but not access to word meaning, unlike the other tasks), consistent with the conclusion that this region is crucial for pan-modal semantic processing. mri_report_text: All patients underwent MRI scans with volumetric high-resolution T1-weighted images. No significant differences in MRI findings between ICA and noICA groups.
eeg_report_text: Each patient underwent a video-EEG long-term monitoring (VLTM) with a 10-20 EEG system integrated with a standard precordial single-channel electrocardiogram (EKG), pulse-oximetry for SpO2 measurement, and a thoracoabdominal belt for respiratory inductance plethysmography.

semiology_text: Gelastic seizures (GS) followed by additional seizure types (FIAS, FBTCS)
mri_report_text: Diagnosis of HH based on T1-weighted, T2-weighted, and fluid-attenuated inversion recovery MRI
eeg_report_text: Synchronous rhythmic spikes/sharp waves across the HH and prefrontal cortex (pattern D); ictal discharges within the HH always started with abrupt giant shifts superimposed on low-voltage fast activity

mri_report_text: PNH were located along the frontal horns, anteriorly, and the temporal horns and trigones, posteriorly. Additional cortical gyration anomalies were present. Topology of heterotopic nodules: Bilat frontal horns (L:1, R:5); bilat, extending from trigones to temporal horns (L:1, R:1). Total no. of nodules: 8.
eeg_report_text: Scalp EEG findings: Interictal: Independent IED over L F‐T, L T or bilateral PQ regions. Ictal: onset max over L T region. Location/number of nodules implanted with stereo‐EEG: L trigone~temporal horn/1 L frontal horn/1 R trigone~temporal horn/1 R frontal horn/1.

semiology_text: Generalized tonic–clonic seizures since 14 months. Currently focal and generalized seizures, relapsing frequently and necessitating adjustment of medication.
mri_report_text: Macrocephaly with frontal bossing and squared appearance of frontal bone. Dysmorphic callosum and basal ganglia. Small pontine volume.
eeg_report_text: Longitudinal EEGs show features of progressive epileptic encephalopathy with increasingly frequent multifocal discharges and frequent, often long, clinical and electrographic seizures with multifocal onset from either hemisphere.
